content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 18, 1994

George A. Pappy
Pappy & Davis
3424 Wilshire Boulevard
Suite 1100
Los Angeles, CA 90010-2258

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-031

Dear Mr. Pappy:

Your law firm represents the Southern California District
Council of Operative Plasterers and Cement Masons International
Association (the "district council") and its political action
committee known as Southern California District Council of the
Operative Plasterers and Cement Masons International Association
Political Action Committee (the "PAC").

You are requesting advice on behalf of the district council
regarding the "personal use" provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

May the PAC refund contributions to the district council, its
sponsoring organization, to pay for its operating expenses?

CONCLUSION

The PAC may not refund contributions to the district council
for its operating expenses.

FACTS

The district council is a labor organization which engages in
representation of its members in collective bargaining
negotiations and the enforcement of existing collective bargaining
agreements. Recently the district council has experienced
financial difficulties and desires to seek a refund of

___________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-031
Page 2


contributions it has made to the PAC on behalf of its members.
The directors of the PAC are agreeable. The refund will only be
used for the normal business of the district council.

ANALYSIS

The general rule is that an expenditure of campaign funds by
a committee not controlled by a candidate must be, at a minimum,
reasonably "related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose. (Section 89512.5, emphasis added.) The underlying
theory for this rule is that these funds are held in trust by the
committee for certain limited uses.

Pursuant to Regulation 18225, an "expenditure" is any payment
made for political purposes. A payment is made for political
purposes if, in part, it is made by:

*****

(D) An organization formed or existing primarily
for political purposes as defined in subsection (a)(1),
including but not limited to a political action
committee established by any membership organization,
labor union or corporation.

Regulation 18225(a)(2)(D)
(Emphasis added.)

The PAC would like to refund contributions to the district
council for its normal operating expenses. You contend that this
would be a refund² and not an expenditure within the meaning of
Section 89512.5, since the payment is not for a political purpose.
We do not agree.

By definition, an expenditure or payment is for a political
purpose if it is made by a PAC, and Section 89512.5 specifically
states that an expenditure made by a committee must be reasonably
related to a political, legislative or governmental purpose. In
our opinion, a transfer of PAC monies to the district council does
not qualify as a political, legislative or governmental purpose.
(Brown Advice Letter, No. I-90-412, copy enclosed.)


2  A "refund" presumes that the district council made the
contribution. In actuality, the council merely acts as an
intermediary for its members who made voluntary contributions to
the PAC through a dues "check-off" or other method.

File No. A-94-031
Page 3


I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: [illegible]
Jim Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

Enclosure

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May the PAC refund contributions to the district council, its
sponsoring organization, to pay for its operating expenses?

CONCLUSION: The PAC may not refund contributions to the district council
for its operating expenses.
